prompt_md: A document API must support three roles: members read only documents they own, team leads read any document owned by their team, and admins read everything. The current code only checks `document.owner_id == user.id`, which wrongly blocks leads and admins. A developer proposes simply skipping the ownership check for any staff account. What should the team do instead?

decision_choices: Skip the ownership check for all staff accounts as proposed
Enforce a rule that grants access by role scoped to the correct relationship (own document, own team, or admin), failing closed otherwise
Remove the ownership check entirely and rely on the UI to hide documents
Keep owner-only and tell leads and admins to request documents manually
reason_choices: Because correct authorization must combine role and relationship and deny anything not explicitly permitted, so leads cannot reach other teams' documents
Because any logged-in staff member can be trusted with all data
Because hiding documents in the interface is an adequate access control
Because owner-only is the most secure rule even if it blocks legitimate access
